data.title: Is Velo3D’s (VELO) Army 3D-Printing Pact Reframing Its Defense-Focused Investment Narrative?
data.description:
• Velo3D, Inc. recently entered into a Cooperative Research & Development Agreement with the U.S. Army DEVCOM Ground Vehicle Systems Center to rapidly develop and qualify additively manufactured parts and assemblies for ground combat vehicles and other military systems.
  • This collaboration underscores Velo3D's role in addressing defense supply chain bottlenecks by using its U.S.-assembled Sapphire printers and Rapid Production Solution to produce larger, mission-critical components with in-situ process monitoring and DoD-compliant cybersecurity.
  • Next, we will examine how this U.S. Army collaboration and its focus on mission-critical additive manufacturing affects Velo3D's investment narrative.

Rare earth metals are the new gold rush. Find out which 32 stocks are leading the charge.

Advertisement

What Is Velo3D's Investment Narrative?

To own Velo3D, you have to believe its metal 3D printing platform can become embedded in mission‑critical defense and space supply chains, despite heavy losses and ongoing dilution. The new U.S. Army CRADA fits that thesis neatly, because it validates the company’s Rapid Production Solution and large‑format Sapphire printers in precisely the kind of high‑reliability applications Velo3D is targeting. In the near term, though, the impact is more about strengthening the pipeline and technical credentials than changing revenue or profitability by itself. Short‑term catalysts still center on execution against the US$50 million to US$60 million 2025 revenue range, stabilizing leadership after the CFO transition, and managing repeated capital raises, including the latest shelf registrations. The CRADA may improve Velo3D’s positioning with defense customers, but it does not erase financing and volatility risks.

However, the same Army collaboration arrives just as dilution and volatility remain front and center for shareholders. Our expertly prepared valuation report on Velo3D implies its share price may be too high.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Three Simply Wall St Community fair value views span from about US$0.07 to US$18, showing sharply different expectations. Set that against Velo3D’s ongoing losses and reliance on fresh equity, and it is clear why opinions on its long‑term potential are so far apart.

Explore 3 other fair value estimates on Velo3D - why the stock might be worth as much as 20% more than the current price!

Disagree with this assessment? Create your own narrative in under 3 minutes - extraordinary investment returns rarely come from following the herd.

  • Our free Velo3D research report provides a comprehensive fundamental analysis summarized in a single visual - the Snowflake - making it easy to evaluate Velo3D's overall financial health at a glance.

Contemplating Other Strategies?

Opportunities like this don't last. These are today's most promising picks. Check them out now:

data.title: A Look At Exponent (EXPO) Valuation After Q3 Beat And Analyst Upgrades

At a share price of $74.26, Exponent has a 30 day share price return of 3.47% and a 90 day share price return of 7.14%. However, its 1 year total shareholder return is an 18.09% loss, suggesting recent momentum has picked up after a weaker longer term stretch.

data.title: Is Brookdale Senior Living (BKD) Pricing Fully Reflecting Its Strong Multi Year Share Price Run?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Brookdale Senior Living's share price still offers value after a strong run, this article walks through what the current market price might be implying about the company.
  • The stock last closed at US$12.95, with returns of 6.1% over 7 days, 18.9% over 30 days, 19.5% year to date, 163.2% over 1 year, 339.0% over 3 years, and 162.1% over 5 years, which has put it firmly on many investors' radar.
  • Recent coverage around Brookdale Senior Living has focused on its position within the US senior living and healthcare space, including how it is responding to demand for assisted living and related services. These themes have helped frame investor expectations and provide context for the share price moves you have seen.
  • Right now Brookdale Senior Living scores 3 out of 6 on our valuation checks, giving it a valuation score of 3. Next we will look at what traditional valuation approaches say about that number, before finishing with a more complete way to think about the company's value.

Brookdale Senior Living delivered 163.2% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Healthcare industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Brookdale Senior Living Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes estimates of a company’s future cash flows and discounts them back to today’s value. This gives an estimate of what the whole business might be worth per share.

For Brookdale Senior Living, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections in $. The latest twelve month free cash flow is a loss of $133.3 million. Analysts provide explicit forecasts out to 2027, with free cash flow of $114.8 million in that year. Beyond that, Simply Wall St extrapolates cash flows, with ten year projections rising to $343.7 million in 2035, all adjusted back to today using a discount rate.

Putting those discounted cash flows together gives an estimated intrinsic value of $18.59 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $12.95, the model implies an intrinsic discount of 30.3%, which indicates the shares are trading below this cash flow based estimate.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Brookdale Senior Living is undervalued by 30.3%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 864 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Brookdale Senior Living.

Approach 2: Brookdale Senior Living Price vs Sales

For companies where earnings and book value are less reliable guides, price to sales, or P/S, can be a useful cross check because revenue tends to be more stable and less affected by accounting choices. Investors often look for a P/S level that lines up with the company’s growth profile and risk, with higher growth and lower perceived risk usually justifying a higher multiple, and slower growth or higher risk pointing to a lower one.

Brookdale Senior Living currently trades on a P/S of 1.00x. That sits close to both the peer group average of 0.95x and the wider Healthcare industry average of 1.31x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Brookdale Senior Living is 0.67x, which is its view of what a “normal” P/S might look like once you factor in items such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, company size and specific risks.

This Fair Ratio is designed to be a more tailored anchor than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for those company specific characteristics rather than assuming all Healthcare stocks deserve similar multiples. With the actual P/S of 1.00x sitting above the Fair Ratio of 0.67x, the shares screen as overvalued on this metric.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/S ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1428 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Brookdale Senior Living Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to think about valuation, and that is through Narratives. These let you attach a clear story, your view on Brookdale Senior Living’s future revenue, earnings and margins, to the numbers behind your fair value estimate.

A Narrative is simply your explanation of what you think is happening at a company, linked to a forecast and then to a fair value. Instead of just seeing a single number, you see the beliefs and assumptions that produce it.

On Simply Wall St’s Community page, used by millions of investors, Narratives are presented as an easy tool where you can compare your own fair value for Brookdale Senior Living with other investors and decide whether the current price around US$12.95 looks high or low relative to those stories.

Because Narratives are refreshed when new information appears, such as news or earnings, your fair value view can update in step. This can help you judge whether the gap between Fair Value and Price is widening or closing, and therefore whether you might want to buy, hold or sell.

For example, one Brookdale Senior Living Narrative might assume a much higher fair value based on stronger long term demand for senior living services, while another might set a lower fair value due to concerns about costs and execution risk.

Do you think there's more to the story for Brookdale Senior Living? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.description:

data.description:

For Nemetschek, the big picture an investor needs to buy into is a high-quality, software-driven business that converts strong margins and cash flows into disciplined reinvestment, selective buybacks and a growing dividend, even after a tough share price year. The recent pullback, despite solid earnings progression and a share price still below both analyst and internal fair value estimates, keeps the spotlight on execution: integrating acquisitions like GoCanvas, sustaining double-digit revenue growth and justifying a premium P/E multiple versus peers. The German Corporate Conference appearances by CEO Yves Padrines and IR head Stefanie Zimmermann do not materially change those short term catalysts, but they do signal that management is actively addressing institutional concerns around valuation, margin trends and capital allocation. The real risk remains that expectations simply outrun delivery.

data.title: Earnings Beat And New Buyback Could Be A Game Changer For S&T Bancorp (STBA)
data.description:
• S&T Bancorp has reported fourth-quarter 2025 results with revenue and net interest income ahead of analyst expectations, solid commercial loan and deposit growth, and an increase in net loan charge-offs tied largely to resolving nonperforming assets.
  • Alongside these results, the board approved a new US$100 million share repurchase program running to February 2027, underlining management’s focus on capital efficiency and shareholder returns while maintaining a strong balance sheet.
  • We’ll now examine how the strong earnings beat and new US$100 million buyback authorization shape S&T Bancorp’s investment narrative.

Find companies with promising cash flow potential yet trading below their fair value.

Advertisement

What Is S&T Bancorp's Investment Narrative?

To own S&T Bancorp, you need to be comfortable with a fairly traditional regional bank story built around steady net interest income, disciplined capital return and tight cost control, while accepting modest growth and bank‑specific credit risk. The latest quarter sits neatly in that frame: revenue and net interest income came in ahead of expectations, commercial loan and deposit books expanded, and management paired a new US$100 million buyback with an already solid dividend record. The catch is the spike in net loan charge‑offs to US$11.0 million, largely tied to resolving nonperforming assets. If that proves to be a clean‑up quarter, the main near‑term catalysts remain earnings resilience and capital return. If charge‑offs stay elevated, though, credit quality quickly becomes the central risk to watch. Yet beneath the earnings beat, one key credit‑related risk deserves closer attention.

S&T Bancorp's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued by 36%. Find out what it's worth.

Two Simply Wall St Community fair value estimates for S&T Bancorp span roughly US$41 to US$65, underlining how far apart individual views can be. Set against the recent jump in net loan charge‑offs and the new US$100 million buyback, that spread reminds you to weigh both capital return and credit risk before deciding where you stand.

data.title: Should Veidekke’s New NOK 460m Bane NOR Rail Contract Reshape OB:VEI Investors’ Infrastructure Thesis?
data.description:
• In January 2026, Veidekke announced it had signed a NOK 460 million (excluding VAT) construction contract with Bane NOR to renew railway tracks across five sections in Eastern Norway, involving 46 kilometres of track, about 90,000 tonnes of ballast and more than 75,000 sleepers.
  • This contract underlines Veidekke’s role as a key contractor in Norway’s rail infrastructure, expanding its portfolio of complex, large-scale civil engineering work.
  • Next, we will examine how winning this extensive Bane NOR rail renewal contract shapes Veidekke’s investment narrative and infrastructure positioning.

We've found 13 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

What Is Veidekke's Investment Narrative?

For Veidekke, the big picture an investor needs to buy into is a mature, cash‑generative Nordic contractor that combines steady infrastructure exposure with an historically generous dividend policy, rather than a high‑growth story. Short‑term, the key catalysts remain order intake quality, margin resilience in a competitive construction market and how well earnings support that rising dividend, which recent analysis flagged as not well covered. The NOK 460 million Bane NOR rail renewal contract fits cleanly into this, reinforcing Veidekke’s infrastructure footprint and visibility on future work, but on its own it does not appear large enough relative to NOK 42.5 billion in revenue to change the near‑term earnings profile materially. Instead, it slightly improves contract mix while the core risks around pricing pressure, execution and dividend sustainability remain in focus. However, one risk in particular is easy to overlook and investors should be aware of it.

Despite retreating, Veidekke's shares might still be trading above their fair value and there could be some more downside. Discover how much.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Five Simply Wall St Community fair value views span about NOK 171.50 to NOK 456.44, underlining how far apart private investors can be on Veidekke. Set against this, the recent Bane NOR win subtly strengthens the contract pipeline while leaving the questions on margins and dividend cover very much alive, which readers may want to weigh against those widely differing valuation opinions.

data.title: CCI Group (TSE:7381) Valuation After ¥4.98b Unrealized Losses And Steady Earnings Guidance
data.description:
Advertisement

Event overview and why it matters for CCI Group

CCI Group (TSE:7381) recently booked ¥4.98b in unrealized losses on held to maturity securities, while keeping its earnings and dividend forecasts unchanged for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026.

This combination of recognized losses and steady guidance puts the focus on how comfortably the group’s core banking and leasing operations, led by Hokkoku Bank, can support its current financial outlook.

See our latest analysis for CCI Group.

CCI Group’s recent recognition of ¥4.98b in unrealized losses comes against a backdrop of strong momentum, with a 30 day share price return of 23.86% and a 47.88% 90 day share price return, while the 1 year total shareholder return sits at 94.17%.

If this kind of rebound has you thinking about what else is moving, it could be a good moment to widen your search with fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With shares up sharply over the past year and CCI Group flagging ¥4.98b in unrealized losses, the key question now is simple: is the stock still undervalued, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Preferred P/E of 20.9x: Is it justified?

Based on our SWS checks, CCI Group’s current P/E of 20.9x sits above both its peer group average of 14.1x and the wider JP Banks industry average of 13.6x. This suggests the market is paying a premium for each yen of earnings at the latest close of ¥976.

The P/E ratio compares the share price with earnings per share, so a higher figure can reflect either stronger earnings quality or higher expectations for future profitability. For CCI Group, the data points pull in different directions, which makes that premium worth unpacking.

On the supportive side, earnings grew 34.3% over the past year, ahead of the Banks industry’s 25.6%. Current net profit margins of 14.6% are above last year’s 10.4%, and the company is assessed as having high quality earnings. At the same time, earnings have declined by 0.6% per year over the past 5 years, Return on Equity sits at a low 4.3%, the value score is 0 out of 6, and our DCF work suggests the shares are trading above an estimate of future cash flow value of ¥544.39. This does not obviously line up with a premium P/E.

Compared with the JP Banks industry average P/E of 13.6x, CCI Group’s 20.9x multiple is strongly higher. This implies the market is currently willing to pay materially more for its earnings than for those of the typical Japanese bank.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

Result: Price-to-earnings of 20.9x (OVERVALUED)

However, the ¥4.98b unrealized loss and low 4.3% Return on Equity show how quickly sentiment could cool if earnings quality or capital strength comes into question.

Find out about the key risks to this CCI Group narrative.

Another view from our DCF model

Our DCF model presents a stricter perspective, with an estimated future cash flow value of ¥544.39 per share compared with the current ¥976 price. On this basis, CCI Group appears expensive, and that gap raises a simple question for you: are you paying up for recent momentum?

Look into how the SWS DCF model arrives at its fair value.

Simply Wall St performs a discounted cash flow (DCF) on every stock in the world every day (check out CCI Group for example). We show the entire calculation in full. You can track the result in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted when this changes, or use our stock screener to discover 864 undervalued stocks based on their cash flows.
